title: OECD Issues Three-Part Pillar Two Guidance: Central GIR Filing Penalty Relief, UTPR Safe Harbour for 52–53-Week Fiscal Years, and Central Record Update
summary: The OECD published a common understanding among 33 implementing jurisdictions providing conditional late-filing penalty relief for MNEs using central GIR filing, provided the return is submitted to any qualifying jurisdiction by the local deadline and a local notification is filed; relief is provisional and lapses if GIR exchange is not completed by 31 December 2026. The package also delivers new Administrative Guidance extending the Transitional UTPR Safe Harbour to MNE groups with 52–53-week fiscal years, and updates the Central Record of jurisdictions with qualifying QIIR/QDMTT status. Vietnam was unable to join the common understanding as of 12 May 2026, introducing group-level GIR strategy risk for MNEs with Vietnamese constituent entities.
razer_relevance: Directly governs the filing strategy for AU (MOL008, CGDMTR/GIR due 30 June 2026) and SG (Razer Online/RMS as Singapore CEs); the Vietnam carve-out from central filing penalty relief must be factored into THS Game JSC's group GIR planning immediately.

title: ATO Opens Pillar Two Lodgment Portal and Issues Account Creation Guidance Ahead of 30 June 2026 Deadline
summary: The ATO published guidance on 19 May 2026 on Pillar Two account and role creation, clarifying that most in-scope entities will have accounts auto-created upon first lodgment, but those nominating separate tax agents for Pillar Two obligations must request account creation in advance. The portal opened on 5 May 2026 for live lodgment of both the GIR (XML schema, OECD 2026 v1.0) and the Combined Global and Domestic Minimum Tax Return (CGDMTR), which are separate obligations and must each be lodged independently. First filings for December 2024 year-ends fall due 30 June 2026, with late-lodgment base penalties scalable up to 500× for significant global entities.
razer_relevance: MOL AccessPortal Pty Ltd (MOL008) is a CGDMTR filer and a potential local GIR lodgment entity; urgent action required to establish ATO portal access, confirm designated local entity role, and validate GIR XML file generation capability with the group's digital service provider before 30 June.

title: Treasury Consults on Draft 2026 Measures No. 2 Rules: One-Year CbCR Safe Harbour Extension and Technical Pillar Two Amendments
summary: Australia's Treasury opened consultation on 1 May 2026 on the Taxation (Multinational—Global and Domestic Minimum Tax) Amendment (2026 Measures No. 2) Rules 2026, proposing a one-year extension to the Transitional CbCR Safe Harbour (allowing MNEs to use simplified CbC-based tests in lieu of a full GloBE ETR calculation) and a package of technical corrections to the existing Australian Pillar Two rules. The consultation window closes before the 30 June first-lodgment deadline, meaning the amending rules may not be enacted before initial filings are due, creating a timing gap for taxpayers seeking to rely on the extended safe harbour. MNEs should assess safe harbour eligibility under current enacted rules and model the extended position as a parallel exercise.
razer_relevance: The CbCR safe harbour extension is directly applicable to MOL008 as an Australian constituent entity; Razer's tax team should determine whether the group qualifies under existing rules and document the position in advance of lodgment, flagging the legislative timing risk to advisers.

title: IRAS Pillar Two Registration Portal Now Live — 30 June 2026 Deadline for December Year-End MNE Groups
summary: The Inland Revenue Authority of Singapore opened its online Pillar Two registration portal in May 2026 for in-scope MNE groups under the Multinational Enterprise (Minimum Tax) Act 2024, covering registration for Multinational Enterprise Top-up Tax (MTT), Domestic Top-up Tax (DTT), and GIR filing obligations. MNE groups with a 31 December 2025 year-end must register by 30 June 2026; failure to register attracts a 10% surcharge on any top-up tax payable and IRAS targets a one-month processing time, making early submission critical. The Ultimate Parent Entity must designate a single Singapore constituent entity as the DTT and GIR filing entity, and must disclose all Singapore-located entities including any that shifted tax residency out of Singapore after November 2021.
razer_relevance: Razer Online Pte Ltd and RMS Managed Services Pte Ltd are Singapore constituent entities; if consolidated group revenue meets the €750 million threshold in two of the prior four years, the UPE must register by 30 June 2026 and immediately designate the Singapore GIR filing entity to avoid the 10% surcharge.

title: Indonesia DJP Issues PER-6/PJ/2026: Comprehensive Pillar Two Administrative Procedures Operative via Coretax
summary: Indonesia's Directorate General of Taxes issued Regulation PER-6/PJ/2026 on 4 May 2026, establishing detailed procedures for GloBE taxpayer registration, annual returns (IIR, UTPR, DMTT), GIR filing, notifications, payment codes, post-filing adjustments, audits, and dispute resolution under Indonesia's Pillar Two framework (Regulation No. 136 of 2024). Covered taxpayers must apply for GloBE taxpayer status through the Coretax portal no later than nine months after the end of the first GloBE imposition year; the DGT may register entities ex officio and may supervise and audit entities that have not self-registered, removing any compliance benefit from non-registration. The GloBE annual return filing deadline is four months after year-end, with a two-month first-year extension available on application.
razer_relevance: PT MOL AccessPortal is an Indonesian constituent entity subject to DMTT (from FY2025) and UTPR allocation (from FY2026); PER-6 makes registration, Coretax filing, and coordination with the group's designated filing entity for GIR exchange practically unavoidable — DJP can and will act ex officio if no registration is submitted.
source: Indonesia Directorate General of Taxes (DJP) / Conventus Law

title: Circular 20/2026 Now Operative FCT Framework; Vietnam Excluded from OECD Central GIR Penalty Relief Arrangement
summary: A comprehensive legal alert published 20 May 2026 confirms that Circular 20/2026/TT-BTC (effective 12 March 2026) is the operative instrument for Vietnam's Foreign Contractor Tax regime, publishing a revised schedule of deemed VAT and CIT rates by contract type and sector that both Vietnamese withholding parties and foreign contractors must apply from 12 March; the alert synthesises three successive reform instruments including CIT Law No. 67/2025 (effective October 2025). Separately, Vietnam was confirmed as unable to join the OECD's 18 May 2026 common understanding on central GIR filing penalty relief, reflecting the absence of enacted Pillar Two domestic legislation, though this does not affect Vietnam's participation as an MNE operating jurisdiction for groups filing GIRs elsewhere. The FCT regime continues to expand its digital nexus coverage, capturing SaaS, e-commerce, and digital service payments even without a Vietnamese permanent establishment.
razer_relevance: THS Game JSC (BDO Vietnam-managed) is the Vietnamese withholding agent on cross-border service payments to Razer group entities; the revised deemed rate schedule under Circular 20/2026 must be applied to all intercompany contracts from 12 March 2026 and BDO Vietnam should confirm correct FCT rates are in use across all active payment streams.
source: Duane Morris Vietnam Law Blog (Dr. Oliver Massmann) / OECD Inclusive Framework

title: Japan Qualified Invoice System: JCT Input Tax Credit Transitional Allowance Steps Down from 80% to 50% from 1 October 2026
summary: Under Japan's Qualified Invoice System (インボイス制度), the transitional JCT input tax credit allowance for purchases from non-qualified invoice issuers steps down from the current 80% to 50% effective 1 October 2026, and will be eliminated entirely from 1 October 2029, making vendor qualification status a direct cost driver. Businesses that have not completed supplier registry audits to identify non-qualified issuers will face material JCT leakage starting Q4 2026, particularly in digital content and platform service supply chains. The FY2026 tax reform separately signals platform-level JCT liability for digital marketplace operators on low-value goods sales (under JPY 10,000) from April 2028, though implementation steps commence from April 2027.
razer_relevance: Razer Gold Japan K.K. (RGJKK) is a JCT-registered entity and must complete a vendor invoice registry audit before 1 October 2026 to quantify the incremental JCT input cost from the credit step-down, with priority on digital content providers, payment processors, and platform infrastructure suppliers.
source: EU-Japan Centre for Industrial Cooperation / BDO Global Indirect Tax
